meta.usage: Use patterns[].lineStart/lineEnd to read exact sections with read_file tool

patterns.title: Pattern 2: EIP-4844 Blob Incompatibility Halts Block Processing

patterns.title: Pattern 5: Memory Corruption Causing Incorrect Batch Hashes (Scroll)

patterns.title: Pattern 6: Incorrect basefee Calculation on Taiko Rollup

patterns.title: Pattern 7: inChallenge Incorrectly Reset in revertBatch

patterns.title: Missing PIL / AIR Constraints
Opcode Arithmetic Constraints
RISC-V / VM Circuit Constraints
Hash Circuit Constraints
Vulnerability Description
patterns.rootCause: In ZK proof systems, soundness requires that **every wire and intermediate value be fully constrained** by the circuit's polynomial identities. When a constraint is missing or incomplete, an adversary

patterns.title: Pattern 1: Missing PIL Constraint for SMT Inclusion
patterns.rootCause: storage.pil - Missing constraint on SMT sibling node relationship

patterns.title: Pattern 2: Underconstrained Carry Value in Binary State Machine
patterns.rootCause: binary.pil - carry bit not range-constrained

patterns.title: Pattern 3: Missing Range Constraint on Division Remainder (zkEVM Opcode)
patterns.rootCause: When implementing integer division (`a / b = q, remainder r`) in a ZK circuit, the standard approach allocates witness variables for `q` and `r`, then constrains: `q * b + r = a`. A critical additiona

patterns.title: Pattern 4: IsLtArraySubAir Soundness Issue (RISC-V Circuit)
patterns.rootCause: The `IsLtArraySubAir` circuit is supposed to compare two arrays lexicographically and output `true` if the first is strictly less than the second. The circuit tracks a `diff_marker` that selects the f

patterns.title: Pattern 5: SHA256 AIR Unconstrained final_hash at Last Block
patterns.rootCause: The `sha256-air` processes input data in 64-byte blocks. The `final_hash` column is constrained to the running hash state for all blocks **except the last one**. The `sha256` chip extension then reads

patterns.title: Pattern 6: partial_sha256_var_interstitial Hash Collision (Undersized Input)
patterns.rootCause: `partial_sha256_var_interstitial` hashes `BLOCK_SIZE * floor(N / BLOCK_SIZE)` bytes, not `N` bytes. When `N < message_size` and `N mod BLOCK_SIZE != 0`, the last incomplete block is silently dropped.

patterns.title: Pattern 7: JALR imm_sign Unconstrained (RISC-V zkVM)

patterns.title: Pattern 4: Nonce Doesn't Increment for Reverted Child Deployments

patterns.title: Gas Calculation Errors
Fee Theft and Manipulation
Vulnerability Description
patterns.rootCause: 1. **Pubdata gas not burned**: If `spentOnPubdata` (the gas consumed by L1 pubdata costs) is refunded to users or paymasters instead of being burned, the protocol subsidizes L1 costs from treasury fun

patterns.title: Pattern 1: Paymaster Refunds spentOnPubdata Instead of Burning
patterns.rootCause: In ZKSync, `spentOnPubdata` represents the gas consumed by posting state diffs to L1 (pubdata). This cost must be **burned** (not returned), because the protocol uses it to pay L1 validators. If the p

patterns.title: Pattern 2: Operator Steals All Gas Provided for L1→L2 Transactions

patterns.title: Pattern 3: Gas Calculation Uses Unchecked Free Variables
patterns.rootCause: In STARK/ZK proof systems compiled from high-level languages, some intermediate variables in gas calculations may not be constrained by the proof (they are "free" or "witness" values). If the prover/o

patterns.title: Pattern 4: Burning User Gas in sendCompressedBytecode
patterns.rootCause: When a new contract is deployed on ZKSync Era, the bytecode may need to be compressed and sent to L1 as part of pubdata. The `sendCompressedBytecode()` system function adds pubdata costs. If this func

patterns.title: Pattern 5: Incorrect commitScalar Underpays Sequencer

patterns.title: Pattern 6: Batch Fees Multiplier Cap Bypassed with Multiple Calls

patterns.title: Pattern 7: Bytecode Compression Bypass Completeness Checks
patterns.rootCause: Bytecode compression in ZKSync involves two steps: `convert` (encode dictionary) and `compress` (apply encoding). The completeness check ensures all original bytecode chunks appeared in the dictionary

2. **Weak randomness**: Fiat-Shamir challe
